appDomain

Class Tag

  • java.lang.Object
    • appDomain.Tag

  • public class Tag
extends java.lang.Object
    Tag represents a single XML tag occurrence found while scanning a document. It records the tag's name (used to match opening and closing tags), the exact raw text of the tag as it appeared in the file (used for error reporting), the line number on which it was found, and whether it is a start tag or an end tag.
    Version:
    1.0
    Author:
    Vansh

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Class and Description
      static class  Tag.Type
      Enumerates the two kinds of tags this parser deals with.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      boolean equals(java.lang.Object obj)
      Two tags are considered equal, for the purposes of the parsing algorithm, if they have the same name.
      int getLine()
      Returns the 1-based line number on which this tag was found.
      java.lang.String getName()
      Returns the tag's name, ignoring attributes.
      java.lang.String getRawText()
      Returns the exact raw text of the tag as it appeared in the document.
      Tag.Type getType()
      Returns whether this tag is a start tag or an end tag.
      int hashCode() 
      boolean isReported()
      Returns whether this tag has already been printed as an error.
      void markReported()
      Marks this tag as having already been printed as an error, so it is not reported a second time during final queue reconciliation.
      java.lang.String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this tag, formatted for use in the parser's error output, e.g.

    • Constructor Detail

      • Tag

        public Tag(java.lang.String name,
           java.lang.String rawText,
           int line,
           Tag.Type type)
        Constructs a new Tag.
        Parameters:
        name - The tag's name, without attributes or angle brackets.
        rawText - The exact raw text of the tag, as it appeared in the document.
        line - The 1-based line number on which the tag was found.
        type - Whether the tag is a start tag or an end tag.

    • Method Detail

      • getName

        public java.lang.String getName()
        Returns the tag's name, ignoring attributes.
        Returns:
        The tag's name.

      • getRawText

        public java.lang.String getRawText()
        Returns the exact raw text of the tag as it appeared in the document.
        Returns:
        The raw tag text.

      • getLine

        public int getLine()
        Returns the 1-based line number on which this tag was found.
        Returns:
        The line number.

      • getType

        public Tag.Type getType()
        Returns whether this tag is a start tag or an end tag.
        Returns:
        The type of this tag.

      • isReported

        public boolean isReported()
        Returns whether this tag has already been printed as an error.
        Returns:
        true if this tag was already reported.

      • markReported

        public void markReported()
        Marks this tag as having already been printed as an error, so it is not reported a second time during final queue reconciliation.

      • equals

        public boolean equals(java.lang.Object obj)
        Two tags are considered equal, for the purposes of the parsing algorithm, if they have the same name. Case sensitivity is preserved since XML tags are case-sensitive.
        Overrides:
        equals in class java.lang.Object
        Parameters:
        obj - The object to compare against.
        Returns:
        true if obj is a Tag with the same name.

      • hashCode

        public int hashCode()
        Overrides:
        hashCode in class java.lang.Object

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        Returns a string representation of this tag, formatted for use in the parser's error output, e.g. "line: 8 <i>".
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object
        Returns:
        A formatted string describing this tag's line and raw text.
